mnemonics: CRAWL - Crawl, Retrieve, Assess, Weigh, Locate - the core IR process
keyPoints: Understanding the core components of an IR system (crawler, indexer, retrieval models)
Ability to compare and contrast different retrieval models (Boolean, Vector Space, Probabilistic)
Knowledge of evaluation metrics for IR systems (Precision, Recall, F-Measure)
commonMistakes: Confusing Precision (correct results from retrieved set) with Recall (all correct results retrieved)
Applying Boolean model operators incorrectly (e.g., misusing AND/OR/NOT logic)
Forgetting that TF-IDF measures importance, not just frequency, within a document collection
